name: BPC-157
description: Any pharmacological substance which is not addressed by any of the subsequent sections of the List and with no current approval by any governmental regulatory health authority for human therapeutic use (e.g. drugs under pre-clinical or clinical development or discontinued, designer drugs, substances approved only for veterinary use).

description: Any pharmacological substance which is not addressed by any of the subsequent sections of the List and with no current approval by any governmental regulatory health authority for human therapeutic use (e.g. drugs under pre-clinical or clinical development or discontinued, designer drugs, substances approved only for veterinary use).

name: Formoterol
description: Inhaled formoterol is permitted subject to a maximum delivered dose of 54 micrograms over 24 hours. Doses must not exceed 36 micrograms over 12 hours starting from any dose.

urineThreshold.description: Presence in urine in excess of 40 ng/mL is not consistent with therapeutic use and will be considered an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF).

name: Salbutamol
description: Inhaled salbutamol is permitted subject to a maximum of 1600 micrograms over 24 hours in divided doses, not to exceed 600 micrograms over 8 hours starting from any dose.

urineThreshold.description: Presence in urine in excess of 1000 ng/mL is not consistent with therapeutic use and will be considered an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF).

name: Vilanterol
description: Inhaled vilanterol is permitted subject to a maximum of 25 micrograms over 24 hours.

name: Activin A-neutralizing antibodies
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation

name: ACE-031
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation
description: A decoy activin receptor, which is an Activin receptor IIB competitor.

name: bimagrumab
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation

name: follistatin
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation

name: myostatin propeptide
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation

name: apitegromab
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation
description: A myostatin- or precursor-neutralizing antibody (Myostatin inhibitor).

name: domagrozumab
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation
description: A myostatin- or precursor-neutralizing antibody (Myostatin inhibitor).

name: landogrozumab
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation
description: A myostatin- or precursor-neutralizing antibody (Myostatin inhibitor).

name: stamulumab
subcategory: S4.3 Agents preventing activin receptor IIB activation
description: A myostatin- or precursor-neutralizing antibody (Myostatin inhibitor).

name: AICAR
description: An activator of the AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK).

name: mitochondrial open reading frame of the 12S rRNA-c
description: An activator of the AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK).

name: 2-(2-methyl-4-((4-methyl-2-(4-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)thiazol-5-yl)methylthio)phenoxy) acetic acid
description: A Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor delta (PPARδ) agonist.

name: thiazides
description: Includes, for example, bendroflumethiazide, chlorothiazide and hydrochlorothiazide.

name: dorzolamide
description: A carbonic anhydrase inhibitor that is not prohibited when administered by topical ophthalmic route.

name: brinzolamide
description: A carbonic anhydrase inhibitor that is not prohibited when administered by topical ophthalmic route.

name: felypressin
description: Not prohibited when used via local administration in dental anaesthesia.

name: Cocaine
description: Designated as a Substance of Abuse.

urineThreshold.description: Prohibited when its concentration in urine is greater than 5 micrograms per millilitre.

name: Cathinone and its analogues
description: Includes analogues such as mephedrone, methedrone, and α-pyrrolidinovalerophenone.

urineThreshold.description: Prohibited when its concentration in urine is greater than 10 micrograms per millilitre.

name: Epinephrine
description: Not prohibited in local administration (e.g. nasal, ophthalmologic) or co-administration with local anaesthetic agents.

urineThreshold.description: Prohibited when its concentration in urine is greater than 10 micrograms per millilitre.
Prohibited when its concentration in urine is greater than 150 micrograms per millilitre.

name: Imidazole derivatives (topical)
description: Derivatives for dermatological, nasal, ophthalmic or otic use are not Prohibited Substances and are included in the 2025 Monitoring Program. Examples: brimonidine, clonazoline, fenoxazoline, indanazoline, naphazoline, oxymetazoline, xylometazoline.

name: Diamorphine
description: Designated as a Substance of Abuse.

name: Tetrahydrocannabinol
description: Natural and synthetic tetrahydrocannabinols (THCs) are prohibited. THC is designated as a Substance of Abuse.

name: Synthetic cannabinoids
description: Synthetic cannabinoids that mimic the effects of THC are prohibited.

name: Cannabidiol
description: Cannabidiol (CBD) is not a Prohibited Substance.

name: Beclometasone
description: Prohibited In-Competition when administered by any injectable, oral (including oromucosal), or rectal route. Other routes (e.g., inhaled, topical) are not prohibited.
